title: HR Executive
description:
Chen Su Lan Methodist Children's Home is a dedicated non-profit organization committed to providing a nurturing and supportive environment for vulnerable children. Rooted in Christian values, our mission includes holistic care, guidance, and spiritual nurturing to help every child and family to shine. We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced HR Executive to join our team and support our HR functions as we continue our mission of transforming lives.

Why Join Us:

Competitive pay: Motivating financial package and regular bonuses.
Competitive benefits and wellness package
Career growth and Development
Support Work Life Balance
And so much more


Job Summary:

As an HR Executive, you will play a vital role in supporting and implementing HR policies, managing employee relations, assisting with recruitment and onboarding, and ensuring compliance with labor laws and company policies. This is a fantastic opportunity to contribute to a meaningful cause and be part of a compassionate team.

For candidates with higher qualifications and extensive experience, a Senior HR Executive position will be offered

Key Responsibilities:

  • Assist in developing and implementing HR policies and procedures.
  • Support recruitment efforts, including job postings, candidate screening, and interview coordination. Make salary proposals for new hires.
  • Manage onboarding and orientation programs for new employees.
  • Oversee staff offboarding processes.
  • Handle employee relations, addressing grievances, and ensuring workplace harmony.
  • Monitor and ensure compliance with labour laws and company policies.
  • Support in the annual performance review and compensation review exercise.
  • Manage staff insurance enrollment, review, and termination.
  • Coordinate employee professional development sponsorship.
  • Manage internship programs by liaising with institutes and stakeholders.
  • Maintain HR records and ensure accurate documentation.
  • Act as a secretary or secretariat for the HR sub-committee.
  • Assist in fostering a positive and inclusive work culture.
  • Any other ad-hoc HR tasks and projects as assigned.

Requirements:

  • Diploma in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in HR roles.
  • Strong knowledge of Singapore labour laws and HR best practices.
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
  • Ability to handle sensitive information with confidentiality.
  • Strong problem-solving and organisational skills.

What are you waiting for:

Apply now and join us today with a dedicated team committed to making a difference in the lives of vulnerable children and families. You’ll have the opportunity to lead impactful initiatives, collaborate with passionate colleagues, and contribute to meaningful community-driven solutions. We work closely with community partners to bring holistic support and care to the children and families. Together we make a difference on the lives of the children in the Home. Together We Nurture Every Child and Family to Shine.

If you are a proactive, detail-oriented HR professional looking to make a difference and advance your career, we encourage you to apply! Candidates with higher calibers and experience will be considered for the Senior HR Executive role.

Only shortlisted applicant will be notified.

Interested applicants may send their CV via this portal OR to hr@cslmch.org.sg. (You only need to choose one of these channels to submit your CV.)

postedCompany.description:
Mission

The Mission of the Home is to provide loving, Christian nurture for needy and disadvantaged children of all races and religions, so as to prepare them to be happy and responsible members of the family and community.

 

 

 

Vision

To be a premier Children’s Home and Centre where every child is nurtured to his/her full potential, ingrained with good Christian character to be happy and responsible members of their respective families and the community at large.

 

 

Goals

1.  To develop the Spiritual, Educational, Social, Physical and

Emotional well-being of the children.

2.  To provide an Inclusive Home environment to enhance the

integration of children with special needs and mainstream children.

3.  To assist parents in problem-solving and coping with their

difficulties.

4.  To strengthen Family relationships and enhance the Quality of

family life.
